context.summary: Light up your micro:bit with love by showing a heart.
how_it_works: This is a great first project to learn how to program your micro:bit!
When the program is transferred (flashed) to your micro:bit, the processor steps through the instructions you gave it.
The program shows a heart picture on the micro:bitâs LED display output.
The heart stays on the display as long as it has power because you havenât given it any further instructions to show anything else.
The micro:bit has a set of other built-in images you can use in your projects.
Pictures on computers, phones and tablet screens are made using dots called âpixelsâ. The micro:bitâs display has 25 LED pixels arranged in a 5 x 5 grid.
improve_it: Try choosing other built-in pictures like HAPPY, DUCK or GHOST.
Can you show more than one image?
See if you can make your own pictures by exploring the other projects below.
